I would say WiFi rather than Bluetooth for OTA. WiFi OTA updates work every time for me whereas BLE OTA fails every time.

messages.author: hpux735
messages.text: So, I should say that I'm planning on it being fairly purpose-built. I have over 100 cast aluminum IP67 cases that have holes for N-Connector bulkhead connectors. Because of that, my plan was to have it be fairly plug-and-play. I was planning on designing around LiFePo4 18650 cells for safety. The charging circuit will be based on this design (and therefore the repeater will also be CC/by-sa). Which includes 4.5-28v input voltage range, settable by MPPT resistor. https://www.tindie.com/products/silicognition/lifepo4weredsolar1/
messages.content_html: So, I should say that I'm planning on it being fairly purpose-built. I have over 100 cast aluminum IP67 cases that have holes for N-Connector bulkhead connectors. Because of that, my plan was to have it be fairly plug-and-play. I was planning on designing around LiFePo4 18650 cells for safety. Which includes 4.5-28v input voltage range, settable by MPPT resistor. https://www.tindie.com/products/silicognition/lifepo4weredsolar1/

messages.author: hpux735
stretch ZL3XL
messages.text: Don't reinvent the wheel. Build on, and improve what others have already done. Take a look at WashTastic: https://m.elecrow.com/pages/shop/product/details?id=208392& https://github.com/valzzu/meshtastic-pcbs/tree/main/WashTastic And also the Voltaic Enclosures MPPT board that handles any/all battery chemistries: https://www.etsy.com/ca/listing/1609406536/mppt-solar-battery-charger-for-iot? - the builder is @KeithMon on the Meshtastic Discord. And also the https://lectronz.com/products/solar-mppt-charger-for-meshtastic Wishlist: • Wide input voltage range • MPPT • 5V 3A Boost/Buck converter to be able to drive other stuff, not just the node. • Multiple battery chemistries • All peripherals broken out (SPI/UART/I²C/QWIIC etc)
messages.content_html: Don't reinvent the wheel. Build on, and improve what others have already done. Take a look at WashTastic: https://m.elecrow.com/pages/shop/product/details?id=208392& https://github.com/valzzu/meshtastic-pcbs/tree/main/WashTastic And also the Voltaic Enclosures MPPT board that handles any/all battery chemistries: https://www.etsy.com/ca/listing/1609406536/mppt-solar-battery-charger-for-iot? - the builder is @KeithMon on the Meshtastic Discord. And also the https://lectronz.com/products/solar-mppt-charger-for-meshtastic Wishlist: • Wide input voltage range • MPPT • 5V 3A Boost/Buck converter to be able to drive other stuff, not just the node. • Multiple battery chemistries • All peripherals broken out (SPI/UART/I²C/QWIIC etc)

meshtastic-pcbs/WashTastic at main · valzzu/meshtastic-pcbs
PCBs i've made for meshtastic. Contribute to valzzu/meshtastic-pcbs development by creating an account on GitHub.

Solar Mesh Baseboard
Solar Mesh Baseboard with RAK4630 (868 and 915 MHz) and MPPT charger.

messages.author: Buzzard
messages.text: Option/pins to add peripherals such as an RTC or sensors integrated mptt function nrf52840 based (most important for me) 868Mhz support Undervoltage protection
messages.content_html: Option/pins to add peripherals such as an RTC or sensors integrated mptt function nrf52840 based (most important for me) 868Mhz support Undervoltage protection
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: hpux735
messages.text: My plan is to of course not reinvent the wheel, but I have to be careful where I take inspiration. The washtastic is licensed CC, but non-commercial, and the Solar Mesh isn't licensed at all, so you have to assume it's closed source, even if there are schematics provided. Same with the voltaic enclosures projects. Therefore, I'm left with the Ikoka Stick (nRF52840) and probably LiFePo4werd/Solar1 solar charge and battery management controller. For inspiration. This charge controller, while it only works with LiFePo4, has MPPT with a wide temperature range, supports pack heaters and thermal management, and has undervoltage cut-out. The enclosure will be quite tight, so adding peripherals within it will be a challenge, the connector I'm planning on using for the solar panel has 4-pins, so 2 could be solar DC in and 2 could be 5v Out, but that wouldn't leave any ability to communicate back to the device. I understand that these design decisions won't make it the ultimate repeater board, but one thing can't be all things to all people. I could look at putting an RTC chip on the board if it doesn't impact the BOM cost too much. My hope is to include at least one, and possibly two LiFePo4 cells within the enclosure, and have everything fit within one enclosure linked above
messages.content_html: My plan is to of course not reinvent the wheel, but I have to be careful where I take inspiration. The washtastic is licensed CC, but non-commercial, and the Solar Mesh isn't licensed at all, so you have to assume it's closed source, even if there are schematics provided. Same with the voltaic enclosures projects. Therefore, I'm left with the Ikoka Stick (nRF52840) and probably LiFePo4werd/Solar1 solar charge and battery management controller. For inspiration. This charge controller, while it only works with LiFePo4, has MPPT with a wide temperature range, supports pack heaters and thermal management, and has undervoltage cut-out. The enclosure will be quite tight, so adding peripherals within it will be a challenge, the connector I'm planning on using for the solar panel has 4-pins, so 2 could be solar DC in and 2 could be 5v Out, but that wouldn't leave any ability to communicate back to the device. I understand that these design decisions won't make it the ultimate repeater board, but one thing can't be all things to all people. I could look at putting an RTC chip on the board if it doesn't impact the BOM cost too much. My hope is to include at least one, and possibly two LiFePo4 cells within the enclosure, and have everything fit within one enclosure linked above
